title: olearia lepidota
intro: Olearia lepidota, also known as the tree daisy, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and grey-green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, fever, and rheumatism. The leaves and flowers of Olearia lepidota are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive issues and skin conditions.
